transcript: I don't know what you mean, we don't have anything like that.
Like a puffy Twinkie. If you want Twinkies, you can talk to the grocery manager.
Who are you looking for? Twinkie Glidden? I'm sorry, you have the wrong number.
[clears throat] I'm afraid not. Listen, if you'll just... if you'll just bear with me. Um... I'm Twinkie Glidden.
Mmhmm. That still don't say who you were looking for.
How does that grab you though? You gotta admit, that, you know, that flattered you a little bit, though.
Now, who is this, and who are you looking for?
You gotta tell me somethin' first.
Did he get that puffy Twinkie?
Yeah. Um, I was calling for Twinkie Gorilla.
I think you have the wrong number, my dear.
N... no, I don't. Listen, if you'll just see it my way for a minute.
I really don't have time to play games on the phone.
I'm not playing with you here.
There's no such person... living here, so would you--
He said he was going to be there in your yard though, about this hour.
[scoffs] You don't eve... what... what yard?
In your yard though. I mean, you gotta, you gotta... you know...
Oh, okay, so he said he'd be in this yard, and you got the number from him?
Miller? Mueller? I'm sorry. Mueller, I never even heard of it.
You gotta admit that was flattering though, wasn't it?
No, it wasn't. It was... actually, it was obnoxious.
[scoffs] Tell me who this is, first. And we'll talk, and see who in the hell you are.
I need to talk to... to, um... Gorilla Romero.
'[looped]' Uh. Uh. Uh. Uh. Well, I think you're on drugs or somethin', buddy. There ain't no... nobody that lives around here by that name.
Listen, I don't think you're being honest with me, partner.
Well, you wanna come down here and find out, or what? Uh.
